name: Kunal, Christy for Congress
subject: How our campaign is adjusting to coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()

| | Peter,

Public health and safety is of the utmost importance to all of us at Team Christy, especially to Christy.

As you may have heard, we are transitioning all of our events to online or telephone ones. But to be honest -- making this switch comes at a significant cost.

We can't spare any time in making this transition -- we have only 56 days to do everything we can to win this race and defeat Mike Garcia (who has proven he's not a leader we can rely on for this crisis or anything else).

On Friday, while we were closing up our office and helping our campaign staff figure out their work-from-home situations, Mike Garcia was tweeting about "Scary Christy Smith" -- a pathetic attack from a desperate campaign. **This is not a man fit for public office.**

**[With such little time left, we need your help to fully make the switch to our digital campaign operation to make sure Christy wins in just a few weeks. Chip in $5 or more today.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b812/29776869/aec6385/569faa90/3422096382/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTZfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200316_digipivot-full")** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [Express Donate $5](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b812/29776869/aec6385/569faa91/3422096382/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTZfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200316_digipivot-full&express_lane=true&amount=5") | [Express Donate $25](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b812/29776869/aec6385/569faa92/3422096382/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTZfZGlnaXBpdm90LWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_digipivot?refcode=em20_200316_digipivot-full&express_lane=true&amount=25")

\-- Kunal

name: Dr. Manny Sethi
subject: Check-Up with Dr. Manny 3/16
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr Manny for U
body:
Happy Monday!

This past week was a difficult week for our state and our country. I am extremely proud of the leadership that has been displayed by President Trump and Governor Lee during this time when everything seems to be up in the air.

I know many folks are worried about coronavirus (COVID-19) as it has recently started to spread here in Tennessee. I released an eight-point plan to address this dangerous disease. As a doctor, I know how serious this can be, but there is no need to panic. Read my eight point plan here to learn how we can address this global outbreak! [https://www.drmannyforsenate.com/dr-manny-sethi-announces-plan-to-address-the-coronavirus-crisis]

Because of the outbreak; however, I have made the decision to scale down my appearances and campaign events until the virus has slowed its spread [https://www.drmannyforsenate.com/dr-manny-sethi-announces-campaign-steps-to-prevent-against-the-spread-of-coronavirus?fbclid=IwAR3siC2erK0uE9ACMfVRS_Hu9XyDgL4dGSuUs_kXmyYaCiXbX65nV-NUZco]. My team and I will be working remotely right now. We feel this is the responsible action to take to protect our elderly and most at-risk.

I believe how you campaign says a lot about what you'll do when elected. These past few weeks, we've packed in events across the state, and were preparing for a busy week this week. With events postponed across the state, we may be working remotely, but we are not slowing down!

This Thursday, we will be doing a virtual town hall on Facebook, and I hope you'll join! I will be going live on my Facebook page to talk about coronavirus and take any questions you have. Click here to register and submit your questions [https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fdrmanny.ivolunteers.com%2FSurveys%2FVirtual-Town-Hall%3Ffbclid%3DIwAR2Q_0dhsx-c6UUofmS2aR23a8nZsuPL0tQgWjF_wIXChu-0xd_4BkDj3PU&h=AT1yJeC-XMhlIgganz6AB-M3ogFw7C7zjXyZwO5-8t43tKwcxZu53iZ8t7jSAEEdm_9dxn0uGVdFbfTS2iM0zWc0UlGgY49uA-IUmhxT49UfDgFhJeztQr2l3MynsHI1Ba76FBePzmtJZDWuT0QhDWA].

News:

Thanks to the Tennessee Star for highlighting my support of President Trump's leadership during this tumultuous time, and and learn why closing our borders might be the best thing for us to slow the pace of transmission of this virus. [https://tennesseestar.com/2020/03/13/manny-sethi-thanks-president-trump-and-encourages-shutting-down-the-borders-if-pace-of-transmission-increases/]

Also, I had the opportunity to join "Inside Tennessee" on WBIR in Knoxville to discuss coronavirus, healthcare, and my US Senate run. Watch the whole interview here! [https://www.wbir.com/video/news/politics/insidetennesseemannysethi/51-28391ada-fde0-4c40-a4d4-9fc017b8cc29]

Social Media:

I applaud President Trump declaring Sunday a National Day of Prayer. God has a plan for us and I think we can all take comfort in knowing he is in control.

While the virus is forcing us to readjust parts of our lives for the time being, there are many simple precautions we can take to reduce the spread. The best thing we can do is wash our hands! I know a good song to sing while washing.

Follow us on Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/drmannyforsenate/?ref=bookmarks], Twitter [http://twitter.com/DrMannySenate], and Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/drmannysenate/]! Also be sure to tag us on social media for your chance to be featured in our next Check-Up with Dr. Manny.

We are just under 5 months until the primary election on August 6th. I can't do this without your support. Together we can make a different kind of difference!

Dr. Manny

P.S. W [https://manny.revv.co/donate] o [https://manny.revv.co/donate] uld you consider chipping in? We can't do this without you, and every donation helps! [https://manny.revv.co/donate]

Donate [https://manny.revv.co/donate?fbclid=IwAR2yLSpO0qYx1jcigfJ4BMpUvmc1NXyj6vIIK1aH7HHLViRvtus-_M-V9_Y]

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: THANK YOU!!!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LIPINSKI FOR CONGRESS
body:
Dear Peter:

As we arrive at election day, it’s difficult to put this experience into perspective, particularly as we go through this unprecedented healthcare crisis that has left us feeling so uncertain. But one thing that Judy and I are not uncertain about is your support.

Whether it was volunteering for the campaign, hosting a coffee, attending a meet and greet where we had the chance to chat, making a donation, or casting your vote. We could not be more grateful for all of the calls, texts, emails and overwhelming amounts of support that you have poured into this campaign, and into our lives.

Today is the deciding day and we certainly hope that you will get to the polls and rally your friends and family to do the same, if you have not done so already. A vote for reasonable, commonsense leadership is so very important, perhaps now more than ever.

So please, make your voice heard and join in our wave of support for the future of working families in IL03.

Thank you once again,

Team Lipinski

PAID FOR BY LIPINSKI FOR CONGRESS

Whether it was volunteering for the campaign, hosting a coffee, attending a meet and greet where we had the chance to chat, making a donation, or casting your vote. We could not be more grateful for all of the calls, texts, emails and overwhelming amounts of support that you have poured into this campaign, and into our lives. Today is the deciding day and we certainly hope that you will get to the polls and rally your friends and family to do the same, if you have not done so already. A vote for reasonable, commonsense leadership is so very important, perhaps now more than ever. So please, make your voice heard and join in our wave of support for the future of working families in IL03. Thank you once again, Team Lipinski PAID FOR BY LIPINSKI FOR CONGRESS

name: Adem Bunkeddeko
subject: COVID-19 UPDATE: Information on how to stay safe
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kd5niHyj0AVQuDdgprFncVbyyuWIZ9iPWvJK5EPpRaE=/rc3-NpogpYLTbvHq1IJLsyX9FaXDxgAq2wPvUTNSvKw=)

Peter,

From all of us here on Team Adem, we hope this message finds you healthy and safe. We've been monitoring the COVID-19 outbreak closely as our communities become increasingly affected by the spread of this virus, and wanted to share some updates with you, as well as best practices for keeping your family safe during this uncertain and difficult time.

On Sunday, Mayor de Blasio closed New York City's public schools -- a move that educators, union leaders, government officials, and health experts have all deemed necessary to protect our communities. Though schools are closed, **free grab-and-go breakfasts and lunches are available to every student, and can be picked up between 7:30am - 1:30pm outside the main entrance to every school building.**

Additionally, New York State's chief administrative judge issued an **indefinite suspension of all eviction proceedings and pending orders statewide**. Having called for this moratorium myself last week as a matter of public health, I'm especially proud and relieved that every tenant across our city and our state can safely remain in their homes.

Our team is heeding the advice of experts by working remotely, practicing social distancing, and frequently washing our hands and sanitizing surfaces. I hope that you're also following the necessary precautions for your health and the health of others, including:
-Avoiding public spaces and gatherings of 10 or more people
-Monitoring your health closely, and self-isolating if you begin to show symptoms of COVID-19, including a fever, headaches, or shortness of breath
-Washing your hands thoroughly and regularly, and for at least 20 seconds

Remember, the best way to protect others is to act as if you have been exposed to the virus yourself: Avoid coming into contact with others, and try to limit your time spent out in public to essential tasks, such as work (if you cannot work from home), getting groceries and supplies or essential medical care.

This is a rapidly evolving situation, and we know that there can be conflicting information regarding how to best protect yourself and others. If you have questions or concerns, don't know the best place to get any needed help, or are feeling the impacts of social distancing and want to talk to a friendly voice, give our team a call at 732-638-0432. I've also attached some helpful links below, where you can find more information:

New York City Department of Health: **[https://www1.nyc.gov/site/doh/health/health-topics/coronavirus.page](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kd5niHyj0AVQuDdgprFncVbyyuWIZ9iPWvJK5EPpRaE=/Nw1LdFraDS1Y8_mZ7jermbMYBVbyMyXXN1vb2NgN_1k=)**

New York State Department of Health: **[https://www.health.ny.gov/diseases/communicable/coronavirus/](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kd5niHyj0AVQuDdgprFncVbyyuWIZ9iPWvJK5EPpRaE=/ebtup4fQVtRsfldH7NpZfbhIJapi5q-qG3xbsgh2PcY=)**

New York State Department of Health Coronavirus Hotline: 1-888-364-3065

Centers for Disease Control and Prevention: **[https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-nCoV/index.html](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kd5niHyj0AVQuDdgprFncVbyyuWIZ9iPWvJK5EPpRaE=/BqwLRWW3Vhuyu_lNhfTF79klqL6-2s2VKdarf70FkUc=)**

Stay safe,
Adem

Dear Friend, The clownishness of Matt Gaetz, the dangerous sycophancy of Devin Nunes, and the foot-dragging of the Trump administration have needlessly increased the risks all of our families and loved ones face in the current crisis. The longer term impacts of the pandemic will be compounded by decades of irresponsible policy. name: Allen Chen
subject: An Update on COVID-19 And Our Campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/0UpKqlRtPALXSIl447uK8O3-53VvRQG7YhV55nCMFM0=/4WZMduHX4k83Ll0PcXaLXq42RP0MWtY0AJmsK6ERmjo=?refcode=20200313&amount=25)
---
Team, This is a long email, but I hope you'll stay with me through to the end. We're in a difficult time right now, and it's likely to be that way for the next few weeks. Sri has been speaking with our statewide leaders and local elected officials to make sure we're taking the appropriate measures to keep all Texans safe. It's important we get accurate and timely information out to residents so that we can slow or stop the spread of this coronavirus also known as COVID-19. I also want to provide you with a few updates on the campaign. The first and best place to get information is from our [State Department of Health and Human Services](https://act.myngp.com/el/0UpKqlRtPALXSIl447uK8O3-53VvRQG7YhV55nCMFM0=/VWMxTN8WniTMFQ8Wehpb6AiX5aDFs3lHSFHP_eLO3xs=) or the [CDC](https://act.myngp.com/el/0UpKqlRtPALXSIl447uK8O3-53VvRQG7YhV55nCMFM0=/Nzz60-8AQy9Q96prM0dczaSH-5MItlcoqWQOT2O1sDw=), both of which have been posting several updates a day and have the most accurate information available. You can help protect yourself by following these recommendations:
* Cover coughs or sneezes with your elbow. Do not use your hands!
* Wash hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds. Use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer if soap and water are not available.
* Clean surfaces frequently, including countertops, light switches, cell phones, remotes, and other frequently touched items.
* Contain: if you are sick, stay home until you are feeling better.

As much as Sri and I enjoy attending local meetings and speaking to voters across our district, our campaign will not be holding in-person events or knocking on doors for the remainder of the month. **Instead, we are encouraging supporters and volunteers to take action online and do their part to help us #FlattenTheCurve. ** We're launching virtual phone banks, peer-to-peer texting, and some volunteers have asked us to send them postcards to put in the mail while they're at home. Although we may be separated physically for the foreseeable future, we will rise above this all together in our goal to bring true representation to TX-22. Thank you. Allen Chen **** ****

subject: Our movement is people powered
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16191639/171733950/-940294256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OTQzZTc3Yy02YTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k9hWcrqpu7me0uIQGQ3MiYkguolygZLLdX9YTwbZZAs=&emci=c8306517-5b68-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6943e77c-6a68-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=9814644) Hey team, I wanted to reach out and follow up again about our Mid-Month goal. The COVID-19 virus has changed the way that we’re approaching this campaign, which means that we’re putting more emphasis on digital and virtual channels. Our digital ads are going to be more important than ever, since we’re not holding events and restricting face-to-face contact as much as possible. As I’m sure you also know, at the end of this month (just 2 weeks from now), we have our first quarterly deadline of the year, when we have to file our fundraising report with the Federal Election Commission. My opponent isn’t afraid of that, because if his coffers are low, he can just “loan” his campaign boatloads of cash. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
---
Peter,

At 1 a.m. on Saturday, the House came together and overwhelmingly passed the Families First Coronavirus Response Act.

This bill (when passed by the Senate and signed into law) will provide free COVID-19 testing, paid sick leave, expanded food benefits and unemployment insurance, and protections for frontline health care workers.

There were only 40 no votes -- **our own representative, Steve King, was one of them.**

His vote shows how little he cares for the folks in the district. He would rather tout hyper-partisan views than help those who are suffering during this pandemic.

Rural America, especially Iowa, faces unique vulnerabilities to this public health crisis. Our population is older, sicker, and poorer than average. More people are uninsured and under-insured and lack access to healthcare and paid leave. Our few and far between hospitals and clinics could easily be overwhelmed by a large volume of patients.

Steve King has the luxuries that so many of us don’t have: paid leave, great health insurance, and a comfortable, stable salary. Compare that to the folks who work hourly jobs, who have to choose between staying home and healthy and earning a paycheck that will keep the roof over their heads, and who are scrambling to figure out how to take care of their children and older relatives during this time.

**We are safer and stronger when we look out for each other. I promise to always look out for you, Peter. Unfortunately, I can’t say the same about Steve King.**

Please be safe out there and follow all of the recommended precautions by our government and the CDC. We need to take this seriously. Last weekend was the first time I didn’t drink Communion wine at church. This weekend, I attended a virtual mass. **The choices we make now impact so many others -- so please, do the right thing.**

Standing Tall for All,

J.D. Congressional District 5 is filled with so many great people from all walks of life. We cannot allow Kendra Horn to scam another election off of us. It's time we elect a real leader, not a Pelosi follower. I'll stay true to my Oklahoma roots. But I need your help. Thank you for your support. With your help, we’ll get it done together. **The Secretary of State of Kentucky announced that the primary election will now be postponed to June 23 because of the coronavirus pandemic.**

| | Wow, I was blown away by what Brandon and this team did for my birthday!

Y'all are the best team I could ask for, and it means so much to me that there are so many people supporting our fight to create a better future for Texas and the country. Thank you.

I wanted to share a couple of the messages that y'all sent me because I was so touched by them: "Happy Birthday MJ! So grateful that you're fighting for a better state and a better country. I look forward to calling you Senator in the near future."
\-- Aaron from Ropesville, TX

"Thank you for your service to our country and for having the courage and integrity to get in this race without support from special interests. I really hope you win, because Texas needs more representatives like you."
\-- Rachel from McKinney, TX

"Happy Birthday! Thank you for running to represent Texas in the US Senate. We need someone who is looking out for ALL Texans and I believe you are that person."
\-- Alisa from Austin, TX We're going to win this fight because of all of you -- your passion, your energy, and your desire for a better future. We've got a lot of work left to do, but all of your messages harden my resolve that we're gonna return this Senate seat to the people of Texas.

Today, I'm taking all of your inspiring words from the birthday card and working harder than ever to win this fight.

Thanks, y'all.

\-- MJ

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16197844/171793197/664205141?refcode=em031720-header&refcode2=5fee5f8d-8168-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZmVlNWY4ZC04MTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lCMZvyd_RrZElm-Jkg8UtLEyvB_4_Ih0OeBGultGIX0=) **** | | Friend –

Georgia's March 24 presidential primary has been postponed due to CDC guidance, and now, it is scheduled to be held with the United States Senate primary on May 19th.

**That means our final** **quarterly deadline****before voters choose their nominee to run against David Perdue is just two weeks from today.** And right now – more than ever – we see that leadership matters.

There is a national state of emergency as we grapple with a global pandemic. Elections are happening right now to elect the leaders we will entrust to deal with this crisis. Georgia needs someone who is ready for the job on Day One.

**I know the responsibilities and demands mayors and public safety directors are facing right now across America because I was one for eight years.** When I held those positions, we conducted extensive simulations and preparations on how to confront crises just like this one. I hold a national security secret clearance from my work with the military at Ft. Benning - and I brought veterans’ homelessness in Columbus to net-zero by the end of 2018. That’s the kind of record we need to go up against David Perdue and win in November.

**[I’m counting on your donation right now to give us a boost before this FEC deadline. clean_body: Georgia's March 24 presidential primary has been postponed due to CDC guidance, and now, it is scheduled to be held with the United States Senate primary on May 19th. | | | | = ** | | Friend – Georgia's March 24 presidential primary has been postponed due to CDC guidance, and now, it is scheduled to be held with the United States Senate primary on May 19th. That means our final quarterly deadline**before voters choose their nominee to run against David Perdue is just two weeks from today. And right now – more than ever – we see that leadership matters. There is a national state of emergency as we grapple with a global pandemic. Elections are happening right now to elect the leaders we will entrust to deal with this crisis. Georgia needs someone who is ready for the job on Day One. I know the responsibilities and demands mayors and public safety directors are facing right now across America because I was one for eight years. When I held those positions, we conducted extensive simulations and preparations on how to confront crises just like this one. I hold a national security secret clearance from my work with the military at Ft. Benning - and I brought veterans’ homelessness in Columbus to net-zero by the end of 2018. That’s the kind of record we need to go up against David Perdue and win in November. = | If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: = = = Thank you for your support. name: Team Hicks

disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
We should all be very concerned about our neighbors, our families, and ourselves right now.

|

---
| Friends, We should all be very concerned about our neighbors, our families, and ourselves right now. We all have to pull together — we’ve got to make some tough decisions personally to keep our whole community safe. This is the time where we all have to try to flatten the curve so that our hospitals don’t get overwhelmed. We are taking all precautionary measures as suggested by Governor Andy Beshear, and that means we have postponed all upcoming public events. All of us have a duty to be part of the solution and not part of the problem. So, today, we want to ask you to support local organizations who are working to provide vital resources to our most vulnerable populations:
* **God’s Pantry Food Bank** is a critical front-line resource for our neighbors in need. A $25 donation helps them buy 250 pounds of food and deliver it to 400 food pantries and meal programs they support in Central and Eastern Kentucky. [You can donate to God’s Pantry by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16199925/171813978/-933806084?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTAwMmM0NS04OTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFeFccA9rxxtpx2eC7SnmCu7LGAfWXIDJcyxBW6sr3w=&emci=0d02e64a-7868-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=39002c45-8968-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=15601662)

* **Blue Grass Community Foundation** funds organizations and programs that have deep roots in Kentucky and strong experience working to provide residents with access to food, prescriptions and healthcare, and other childcare and basic needs. Your donation to Bluegrass Community Foundation will help those in economically depressed areas hit hardest by loss of work or reduced wages, and school and business closures. [You can contribute to Blue Grass Community Foundation here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16199926/171813979/-1909446250?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTAwMmM0NS04OTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFeFccA9rxxtpx2eC7SnmCu7LGAfWXIDJcyxBW6sr3w=&emci=0d02e64a-7868-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=39002c45-8968-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=15601662)

* **Mutual Aid Lexington** is building a network of folks to support their neighbors at this time. They’re helping coordinate everything from meals and transportation to childcare, eldercare, and pet care. [Fill out this form here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16199927/171813980/-1344793409?fbclid=IwAR3DIeUtcpBhW3fFEPHZ2rtgaK4ewlzAiiBDOHvfByJ5rHzKyWzuD7Fb7UM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTAwMmM0NS04OTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFeFccA9rxxtpx2eC7SnmCu7LGAfWXIDJcyxBW6sr3w=&emci=0d02e64a-7868-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=39002c45-8968-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=15601662) to let coordinators know what help you need and/or what you can give. And if you’re not sure how to help, fill out the form and they’ll connect you with a way to get involved.

**Last, but not least,** if you or someone in your family is experiencing symptoms, please visit [this website set up by Kentucky Health and Family Services](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16199928/171813981/-1493642990?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTAwMmM0NS04OTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFeFccA9rxxtpx2eC7SnmCu7LGAfWXIDJcyxBW6sr3w=&emci=0d02e64a-7868-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=39002c45-8968-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=15601662) or call Kentucky’s COVID-19 hotline at 800-722-5725. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16199929/171813982/-1493642990?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjU5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTAwMmM0NS04OTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFeFccA9rxxtpx2eC7SnmCu7LGAfWXIDJcyxBW6sr3w=&emci=0d02e64a-7868-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=39002c45-8968-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=15601662) From day one, this campaign has been about what’s best for everyday Kentuckians. And though our mission is different in this moment, it’s more important than ever. Thank you for being a part of this team. \-- Team Hicks ** ** | ** **

subject: Coronavirus and The Social Safety Net
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_ Our mailing address is:
body:
I hope you are staying safe and healthy during this unfortunate new normal. We have seen the various precautions we can take to limit the spread of this virus | | | [
](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3QA/ni0YAA/t.305/5CTle2JpR9SZCTSNx0Xt-w/h0/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNIxlrbVX39UzVilwU7sMdOTF5nJNHMs72NsgL7qJuloKLbKEEsFzbP9jfEBIkmmfriejW2FaUlhcb8YL4Wt-2Bu0sOyahctgXqIiMsGhz-2BG-2BUakmcZmL9P6n-2BMWiuxJxFmGhIIW7Hb-2FoPfEQ7sB1aBwEgDl-2FyjA0pXkGnU3rV5dLdXAE8-2FqrAKPuomODIcEFCjSC6CFQoym-2BqLrahUIeCXQ1hiR7xMjuZVGkP0F-2B7smBRDA-3D-3D/NmPF) Friend, **** **** **** I hope you are staying safe and healthy during this unfortunate new normal. We have seen the various precautions we can take to limit the spread of this virus — from washing hands for at least 20 seconds to social distancing — but there is one big issue that hasn’t really been discussed: our inadequate social safety net.
**_[Today, our flagship paper, The Journal News, published my op-ed explaining how the coronavirus pandemic demonstrates the urgent need for universal health care, paid sick leave, and an end to poverty.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3QA/ni0YAA/t.305/5CTle2JpR9SZCTSNx0Xt-w/h1/rg86aZHzBBOHcF3LwCaTiWmV51U-2FZKwmLpK5VZBiFJqpXB3EuGFG-2BzkYuO2XfgaKuPhgebZUqXpDY-2FCwmrqmvThm35-2BVWT0VJyxsoIkPmxOVNLIhIG-2FwY71hnqTk7K15C3v32zsaYlCTObNZC3bO6SIp8MTRPo3Vdf8f0HUVqJmSmYVMD5pfJsPgC4K0HRO-2FliAqn5ZKM6hhrl2jc-2FzRvJmVBnDJUx-2BKNnFxewDHjBrC8IClZQTBqvWehrwuzBE5qfPRmkPMmQKCN9Z2Buiwd-2B-2B1vSZc-2FW1QQ0MKHbtulsNH-2Bi0IL-2Ba0FtbT70OYcLZuj1GhF-2FrYCysPnRwN1IP1nfQzb0V8o5COEEqmw3PSODo-3D/93bJ)_** In the midst of a growing public health crisis, it is unacceptable that 87 million Americans are either uninsured or underinsured when it comes to accessing necessary health care. Likewise, it exacerbates our public health crisis that sick people have to go into work because federal law does not require employers to provide paid sick leave. And it is unconscionable that food-insecurity and homelessness have caused so many young people to rely on schools being open to receive their basic human necessities. These inadequacies in the social safety net impact all of us, because we are only as healthy as the sickest person among us.
As the coronavirus has impacted our daily lives, it has changed the course for this campaign as well. Since we have stopped petitioning and knocking on doors, we are adapting our field strategy to a more digital effort and need your help. **_[If you share this op-ed on Facebook, Twitter, and via email, that’s already getting our message out to hundreds, if not thousands, of people.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3QA/ni0YAA/t.305/5CTle2JpR9SZCTSNx0Xt-w/h2/rg86aZHzBBOHcF3LwCaTiWmV51U-2FZKwmLpK5VZBiFJqpXB3EuGFG-2BzkYuO2XfgaKuPhgebZUqXpDY-2FCwmrqmvThm35-2BVWT0VJyxsoIkPmxOVNLIhIG-2FwY71hnqTk7K15C3v32zsaYlCTObNZC3bO6RmUhsctKwtRqIsSkMCXdgzbeTiJEhxDL41aoiL2c344T-2FJqG8zR3w4pvfqHBgGvtt1RwFH0TUJOlZ8bOQXvQaZ1Kg9pNRvdOFL-2BMV6AKdZGBAV4RKkaDtgTSmJf0S-2B8cxDUxhH7mjCdTmbpET0wVgyecBgZVIjWSGq9Y5a87Dz4HPa79Kfq8SDpkXRAlEHKNmzFgxAkyDT7S2kPMEX1HwM-3D/BOde)_**
Unlike one of our opponents, we can’t afford to go on TV and spend $100,000 on ads this far out from the election. Instead, we rely on people like you who believe in this campaign and want to share our message. **_[To do so, you can sign up to be a social media organizer and use your networks to get our message out by sharing other content I post.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3QA/ni0YAA/t.305/5CTle2JpR9SZCTSNx0Xt-w/h3/I3pn4SJUQx-2BiuYuahoeeGpCNwsA61TlCqR0-2BMwBys1He-2FuqUjF3anrvAcxNTdtWqqEpR6O1Lkte6ZjgzAcEQ86vb0vu2-2BsxQr7GgeF-2BwVZ3taZAQdO3-2FZaP4CKcqny1FTPg2x2phS1ezKB7MqrtLz6RPbsbY2l-2Fior-2BrbjcAJU4Rs05OVXSK3t7NvvbQrnY9eoDCodM4ZC8-2BJHCDJYYkDadOxzL6EZlL1QG4zgSSVmqyiT7ntxmPCQNvTCJTU7TJrPLCILG-2FArgEeX76q5IWpg-3D-3D/GYLt)_**
Even though the campaign is changing, we’re still working just as hard to win this thing on June 23rd. With your help, we’ll get there. Stay safe and healthy!

clean_body: I hope you are staying safe and healthy during this unfortunate new normal. We have seen the various precautions we can take to limit the spread of this virus | | | Friend, ** I hope you are staying safe and healthy during this unfortunate new normal. We have seen the various precautions we can take to limit the spread of this virus — from washing hands for at least 20 seconds to social distancing — but there is one big issue that hasn’t really been discussed: our inadequate social safety net. In the midst of a growing public health crisis, it is unacceptable that 87 million Americans are either uninsured or underinsured when it comes to accessing necessary health care. Likewise, it exacerbates our public health crisis that sick people have to go into work because federal law does not require employers to provide paid sick leave. And it is unconscionable that food-insecurity and homelessness have caused so many young people to rely on schools being open to receive their basic human necessities. These inadequacies in the social safety net impact all of us, because we are only as healthy as the sickest person among us. As the coronavirus has impacted our daily lives, it has changed the course for this campaign as well. Since we have stopped petitioning and knocking on doors, we are adapting our field strategy to a more digital effort and need your help. Unlike one of our opponents, we can’t afford to go on TV and spend $100,000 on ads this far out from the election. Instead, we rely on people like you who believe in this campaign and want to share our message. Even though the campaign is changing, we’re still working just as hard to win this thing on June 23rd. With your help, we’ll get there. Stay safe and healthy! Sincerely, Mondaire -** | | Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_ Our mailing address is: Mondaire for Congress P.O. subject: Amid Coronavirus Pandemic, a Model of Bipartisanship
body:
"This week, one of President Trump’s most outspoken critics, Gov. Gavin Newsom of California, said this about the president..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eec63b664c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Amid Coronavirus Pandemic, a Model of Bipartisanship**

“This week, one of President Trump’s most outspoken critics, Gov. Gavin Newsom of California, said this about the president and his team’s response to the cruise ship in Oakland: ‘Every single thing [Trump] said, they followed through on. Every single thing. His administration—and it starts at the top, including the vice president—has been consistent,’” Reps. Roger Marshall (R-KS) and Brian Babin (R-TX) write.

“What a fantastic report and an example of true public service. Both conservatives and liberals should look at that situation objectively and realize that in an era of total polarization in politics, two opposing executives put their differences aside to solve a crisis and save lives.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=033439ca94&e=3ee4985eea)

**MORE:**[Do your part—15 days to slow the spread!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bcc8123baa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “It has been alleged by multiple officials of the Obama administration, including in The Post, that the president and his then-national security adviser, John Bolton, ‘dissolved the office’ at the White House in charge of pandemic preparedness. Because I led the very directorate assigned that mission . . . I know the charge is specious,” Tim Morrison writes in [_The Washington Post_.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d781c23be0&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| During a crisis, it’s more important than ever that journalists share accurate information about the Federal Government’s response. Yesterday, a member of _The New York Times_ editorial board shared a partial quote from President Trump “with the explicit intent of misleading people,” Becket Adams writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a666ffb860&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| By slashing red tape, “the president and his task force have changed the way we respond to viral and pandemic events . . . The strength of America lies in her ability to innovate and problem-solve. The best ideas and solutions come from a government and a private sector that come together for the common good,” Bradley Blakeman writes in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f1d9a06b3b&e=3ee4985eea).
---

# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16204434/171856357/1001356148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTlkMTcwMS05YTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c4Y_q40VsPzatT-y9e8s3eXfHczY2wmpJJK49I_mWnY=&emci=ee9bb2a8-9668-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=399d1701-9a68-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=8780864)
Friends \--

As more cities and states mandate the close of restaurants and bars to slow the spread of COVID-19, low-wage workers and small businesses are facing a significant financial strain.

These Americans should not face the burden of foreclosures or evictions from their home or places of business due to lost wages and revenue during this state of emergency.

**Last night, Governor Gavin Newsom announced an executive order halting all foreclosures and evictions in California. I support this as an important first step toward protecting families and small businesses during this unstable time. However, with nearly half of all Americans living paycheck to paycheck, we must think beyond our immediate community and take action as a nation.**

[**That is why I’m calling on Congress to follow California’s lead and pass legislation to halt all foreclosures and evictions for families and small businesses nationwide. Will you add your name to my petition? I will be sending petition signatures directly to Members of Congress.** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16204436/171856359/1765952491?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTlkMTcwMS05YTY4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c4Y_q40VsPzatT-y9e8s3eXfHczY2wmpJJK49I_mWnY=&emci=ee9bb2a8-9668-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=399d1701-9a68-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=8780864) **[Sign the Petition](https://secure.ngpvan.com/6HRhGqL7w02EAzhkrU06Ng2)** Housing is a human right, and Congress must do everything it can to protect families as we come together and take steps to overcome this crisis.

Thank you, and please stay safe.

_Georgette_

#
If it weren't for the COVID-19 pandemic, we would be out celebrating tonight.

We'd be celebrating the end of the primary season here in Illinois, and looking forward to ramping up for the general election this November.

Instead, we'll be at home watching the results come in on our couches while we all practice social distancing and do our part to flatten the curve.

**But while hundreds of watch parties and celebrations may have been canceled tonight, you can still help us reach our end of the month fundraising goal by[ pitching in $5, 10 or whatever you would have spent on a few drinks tonight! ](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=43705c1607&e=140a9b81e7) **

Your donation will help us move forward once the attention shifts from our primary to the general election. When we flip this seat from red to blue in November, it will be because of YOU.

Dani Brzozowski

## Dear Friend -
Hi everyone! From our house to yours...thoughts and prayers are with all our fellow Americans as together we find our way in this (temporary) new normal of daily life. This video was shot last weekend and will be the last one "in studio" for a while.

body:
Peter, their wild accusations and slander are... * | | |
A message from...
---
John Philip Sousa IV
National Chairman of Stars and Stripes Forever PAC
Formerly the 2016 Committee and National Draft Ben Carson for President Committee
Dear Peter, President Trump continues to keep his campaign promise to **Make America Great Again** for EVERY American (_even those who didn't vote for him_). Despite relentless attacks, obstruction, and resistance from all sides - Trump's presidency has the American economy breaking records, American workers experiencing more opportunities, confidence soaring, and business booming. The unemployment rate under President Trump is the **lowest in history** and this includes Black Americans, Hispanics and other minorities... [](https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SB76qR&mc=99&s=sOSe3V&st=BcJRA2&u=z93TV&y=i&z=Ey78IEu&) **...and minorities are responding!** The number of voter polls are mounting showing that President Trump's approval ratings among minorities is dramatically increasing. In fact, some recent polls now show his approval ratings are 25% among African American voters and 50% among Hispanic voters. The Democrats and the Leftist-Media understand that they can't win if this trend continues and that is why they continue to work to discredit these polls. **It is also why they have stepped up their attacks against him and his conservative allies in Congress.** [](https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SB76qR&mc=99&s=sOSe3V&st=BcJRA2&u=z93TV&y=i&z=EyOqMe&) Their accusations about President Trump and Republicans being racists and bigots are getting L O U D E R & L O U D E R ... [That is why it's critical we continue our work to get the truth out.](https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&lc=SB76qR&mc=99&s=sOSe3V&st=BcJRA2&u=z93TV&y=i&z=EBr4QRV&) Since 2013, we have invested millions of dollars on radio, television and print advertisements, billboards, books and issue guides to minority voters exposing the **race-baiting lies** liberals have been spoon-feeding them about Republicans and our values for decades... And the results have been incredible. | Peter,
You probably have a lot of questions about how to deal with the current situation surrounding COVID-19. Leadership comes with transparency and a desire to get you honest and immediate information. In that spirit, we've compiled some resources for you to help answer some of the most common questions we've heard. We are all Americans and in this together. My goal is to be here for you, your family and your business.
Where do we currently stand?
As of today, Dallas County has reported 28 total positive tests for COVID-19. In order to slow the spread, Dallas has closed all bars, gyms, theaters, playgrounds, golf courses and tennis centers. Some restaurants are still open but only for take-out and delivery - not for dining in.
In addition, public and private community gatherings of over 50 people are banned in Dallas, and the White House has advised people across the country to limit group gatherings to ten or fewer.
If you do not reside in Dallas, please utilize your locality's website and local news organizations for information regarding local closures and policies.
What are the symptoms of COVID-19?
According to the CDC, the following symptoms may appear 2-14 days after exposure.

* Fever
* Cough
* Shortness of breath

What do I do if I feel sick?
Do not panic. Your first call should be to your primary care physician, who will provide further instructions and recommendation. Do not seek care from an emergency room unless you develop emergency warning signs. According to the CDC, emergency warning signs include*:

* Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
* Persistent pain or pressure in the chest
* New confusion or inability to arouse
* Bluish lips or face

*This list is not all inclusive. Please consult your medical provider for any other symptoms that are severe or concerning.
Do not leave your home except in the case of the above emergency warning signs or if you've been instructed by your primary care physician to seek medical care.

What if I own a small business that will be impacted by the various policies put in place?
The United States Small Business Administration is working closely with the states when it comes to small businesses suffering substantial economic injury as a result of the virus. Please follow guidance from the state of Texas and the Small Business Administration when it comes to emergency loans and financial assistance. More information is available here: [https://www.sba.gov/disaster-assistance/coronavirus-covid-19](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001jlGTX6NYM4ZTLJAVw4TlUpnU0c40RqGHTjKIqY8QvFJq55rcP1oI52eXsXM-hTdO4WTM6-CGCZisXJ648rEJEV796v366wsLS3jHKPR1veWTA7mqRJ2QfhKM6SG295oBMLAtEcmXCwMsuO8XD1O8ukq_GzybgXiOivEGeDhDJ5LS9lJ9ZomMVZCEtNqWgSAG&c=jng8PnXY8lT-u7UsfXhpBpDfsM1xRXSVbACh5kRRP7c4wYvfL8zCBw==&ch=1pnyabXGTOxx9Y5pOJrT3-MG7q_lBPBXXawXqTCsBGXjh9vcrCnLSg==)
How can I be helpful to my community?
First, do not be dismissive of this virus or the steps necessary to prevent its spread. Work from home if your employer allows. Even if you personally do not fall in the high-risk category, failure to adhere to public health officials' guidelines can increase the chance of transmission to a high-risk individual. Please see here the CDC's guidelines on protecting yourself from transmission: [https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/prepare/prevention.html](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001jlGTX6NYM4ZTLJAVw4TlUpnU0c40RqGHTjKIqY8QvFJq55rcP1oI52eXsXM-hTdOR9sUCl5PHO32YDnkhBZo-4YGcY7FP0H3wVs1ZydZoUPH0KkMxyRbg19emRsSiOCqozQc4ulGrw7cEUWawq1LiqmwAZCwAbdA5jXKvrqvnf0oIQ9prbzx9nwXxMvwjTQgXo2EOgtAc9bxLcYXzLIQvA==&c=jng8PnXY8lT-u7UsfXhpBpDfsM1xRXSVbACh5kRRP7c4wYvfL8zCBw==&ch=1pnyabXGTOxx9Y5pOJrT3-MG7q_lBPBXXawXqTCsBGXjh9vcrCnLSg==)
Consider ordering take-out or drive-thru from local restaurants who will unfortunately be affected by the temporary restrictions on dine-in eating.
Check on your neighbors, particularly the elderly or vulnerable. If somebody you know feels symptoms, urge them to follow the guidelines outlined above as provided by the CDC.
One of the best things you can do for your community is to share information on social media - however, please be careful that the information you are sharing is accurate and coming from an established source such as the CDC, WHO, or your state and local public health professionals. Check on your neighbors, particularly the elderly or vulnerable. If somebody you know feels symptoms, urge them to follow the guidelines outlined above as provided by the CDC. One of the best things you can do for your community is to share information on social media - however, please be careful that the information you are sharing is accurate and coming from an established source such as the CDC, WHO, or your state and local public health professionals. Here are some links to consider sharing to your networks: CDC COVID-19 Resources Homepage: == State of Texas COVID-19 Communication Toolbox: == Johns Hopkins Map of COVID 19: == Food Resources If you are stocking up at the grocery store, here is a helpful article with some guidelines and suggestions that are both nutritious and versatile: == Here are some grocery stores with curbside or in-store pickup: Kroger == Central Market == Aldi == Walmart == Tom Thumb == North Texas Food Bank: == Minnie's Food Pantry: == Note some items have limits to allow more customers to have access to specific items. It is always a good idea to check with your local store for their updated hours or policies. | As this situation continues to evolve, we will do our best to keep you updated with the latest accurate information. Please remember to follow the established guidelines and to take this threat seriously. Please reach out if our team can be of assistance. Together, I am confident that we will get through this as Texans and Americans always do. | | Genevieve Collins Candidate for Congressional District 32 | == | == == == | | | == | | | Paid for by Collins for Texas, Inc. | | Collins for Texas, Inc. | 6119A Greenville Ave., Ste 423, Dallas, TX 75206 | | Sent by mailto:gdc@gcforcongress.com

name: Hillary Holley
subject: Protecting Georgia's democracy during a global pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
Right now, we are making sure Georgia's democracy is free and fair while we fight this global pandemic, and if you're up to it, we need your help. |
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42a/34a064cc/1b5eea19/702b82c/3635507051/VEsH/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://fairfight.com/")

| | | Friend - We hope you and your community are staying safe during this trying time. **Right now, we are making sure Georgia 's democracy is free and fair while we fight this global pandemic, and if you're up to it, we need your help.** Fair Fight Action supports the recent decision by the Secretary of State to move the March 24 primary to May 19, and we are committed to making sure all ballots that were cast early will be counted. Moving the presidential primary to May 19 will allow more Georgians to make their voices heard.

Fair Fight Action also supports proactively sending absentee ballot request forms to voters. However, we urge the Secretary of State to mail these requests forms to ALL voters -- not just older voters who are disproportionately of one race and one political party. In making it easier to vote absentee, we recommend the following gold standard: Return postage is prepaid; Ballots postmarked on or before Election Day must count; Voters are notified and can cure any signature matching issues; and Community groups can collect and return sealed ballots.

**If you 're able, please take a moment to help us protect democracy:**

1. Can you sign this petition to ask the Secretary of State's Office to mail absentee ballot request forms to ALL voters? The petition can be found [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa42a/34a064cc/1b5eea19/702b82d/3635507051/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://forms.gle/aYYQoqTPc35svdDX6").
2. Can you share the following graphic on social media and demand the Secretary of State follows the gold standard? Sample post below:

I support @fairfightaction's gold standard and demand Georgia's Secretary of State to implement these standards for the May 19th elections
* Ballot requests are mailed to every voter of all ages
* Postage is prepaid
* Ballots postmarked by Election Day count
* Voters are notified and can cure any signature matching issues
* Community groups can collect and return sealed ballots

During this pandemic, Georgians of every age group are vulnerable and need their Secretary of State to make voting more accessible. As our state comes together to fight COVID-19, equal access for every voter is essential.

Thank you,

Hillary Holley
Organizing Director, Fair Fight Action
---

|
---

name: Joe Kennedy III
subject: If you are feeling helpless stuck at home, there is still a way you can help others in need.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639002d/55b66746/2333e25f/80e2b4a/1426075223/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | I hope you and your family are holding up ok. I know it is incredibly disorienting, uncertain, and scary right now. And a lot of us feel helpless - particularly as we're stuck at home, unable to physically be with neighbors and friends who may be suffering or struggling. But here's one, simple thing we can all do: Donate to the frontline organizations doing the hard work of protecting the most vulnerable in the face of this crisis. It doesn't have to be a lot - just a few dollars can make all the difference to the groups trying to ensure that no one goes hungry, without a roof over their head, or without access to basic health care during this global pandemic. As most of you know, we temporarily shut down campaign activities in the midst of this crisis - that includes fundraising for ourselves online. Instead, we are going to leverage the power of this email list to help those most in need. | **Donate to Meals on Wheels and Feeding America** Ensure the elderly and the hungry across the country do not go starving in the midst of this pandemic. [DONATE](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639002d/55b66746/2333e25f/80e2b4b/1426075223/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiI3In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-covid?refcode=2020-03-17-charities&recurring=1&amount=7")
---
I am working to do everything I can on Capitol Hill to provide support during this crisis. But organizations like Meals on Wheels and Feeding America are on the front lines, delivering immediate assistance where it is most needed.
Let's show them we stand with them. Let's put the full force of this community into helping each other get through these difficult times.
I'm grateful.
More soon,

**NOTE:** We have temporarily suspended all political activities in the wake of COVID-19. For more information visit **[KennedyForMA.com/COVID19](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639002d/55b66746/2333e25f/80e2b54/1426075223/VEsF/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/covid19/")**
**INFORMATION ABOUT DONATIONS****:** All donations to the links above will be contributed directly to the charities mentioned. Kennedy For MA receives no financial benefit from these donations.
Meals on Wheels America supports more than 5,000 community-based programs across America that deliver nutritious meals, friendly visits, and safety checks that enable seniors to live nourished lives with independence and dignity. MealsOnWheelsAmerica.org

It doesn't have to be a lot - just a few dollars can make all the difference to the groups trying to ensure that no one goes hungry, without a roof over their head, or without access to basic health care during this global pandemic. As most of you know, we temporarily shut down campaign activities in the midst of this crisis - that includes fundraising for ourselves online. body:
When President Trump took the podium at yesterday’s Coronavirus briefing, he asked Americans to unite behind a single goal. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=91508b7865&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 17, 2020
---
| | |
# 🇺🇸 24 hours of all-American teamwork

**When President Trump** took the podium at yesterday’s Coronavirus briefing, he asked Americans to unite behind a single goal. “We’re announcing new guidelines for every American to follow over the next 15 days as we combat the virus,” he said.

**And in just 24 hours** , the selflessness and patriotism on display all across our great country has been astounding.

**The new guidelines** [released yesterday](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=25cf5edd0a&e=3ee4985eea) deliver a nationwide game plan. Each one of us has a role to play in stopping transmission of this virus. Simple steps taken now will have a massive impact down the road, saving many lives in the weeks ahead:
* **_Avoid crowds_** of more than 10 people

* **_Wash your hands_** frequently and thoroughly

* **_Work from home_** if that’s an option for you

* **_Disinfect_** frequently touched surfaces

* **_Isolate_** yourself if you feel sick

**While every American** does their part, the White House Coronavirus Task Force is coordinating a nationwide response to protect the health and safety of every community.

🎬 **NEW:** [FDA is empowering states to expedite testing](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7fcfba8957&e=3ee4985eea)

**To cut red tape** and avoid bureaucratic delay, “we are putting in place a policy for states to take responsibility for tests developed and used by laboratories in their states,” the President announced today. “States can set up a system in which they take responsibility for authorizing such tests and the laboratories will not engage with the FDA.”

**The** **President also** **announced** that Medicare telehealth services will be dramatically expanded. “Medicare patients can now visit any doctor by phone or videoconference, at no additional cost, including with commonly used services like FaceTime and Skype—a historic breakthrough,” he said.

**Our seniors** , as well as others most vulnerable to contracting the virus, shouldn’t have to travel to a doctor’s office or a hospital if another solution is available. Now, the extra fees typically associated with telehealth services have been removed.

🎬 **WATCH:** [Medicare will dramatically expand telehealth services](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9e6417eb53&e=3ee4985eea)

**True to an “all-of-America”** response to the crisis, President Trump is continuing to combine the full resources of the Federal Government with the innovation of America’s world-leading private sector. This morning, he spoke by phone with restaurant executives from the fast food industry, who intend to keep feeding their communities through drive-through, pick-up, and delivery service options in the weeks ahead.

**A few hours later,** he held a phone call with industrial supply retailers and wholesalers to discuss how to ensure supply chains are able to meet the demands of healthcare providers, first responders, and the American people at this critical time.

🎬 **WATCH:** [We applaud stores for creating seniors-only shopping times!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a871b00da4&e=3ee4985eea)

**In addition** to public health concerns**,** President Trump understands the serious challenge this virus presents to American businesses, workers, and families. In addition to steps already taken, such as declaring a National Emergency to free up Federal resources, the Trump Administration is working with Congress on a significant economic stimulus plan to provide relief to American workers and industries.

**Treasury Secretary** Steven Mnuchin also shared today that President Trump has authorized the deferral of $300 billion in tax payments to the IRS, helping American workers and business affected by closings and other economic effects of the pandemic.

“The world is at war with a hidden enemy,” [President Trump tweeted](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e3143ed0a8&e=3ee4985eea "https://twitter.com/realDonaldTrump/status/1239997820242923521"). “WE WILL WIN!”

🎬 [Relief for American workers, industries, and families is on the way!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cc5c51484d&e=3ee4985eea)

**STAY INFORMED:**[For the next 2 weeks, we can all do our part](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2ebab4cd5a&e=3ee4985eea)
---

Peter,
I wanted to take a minute of your time to bring you up to date (https://www.facebook.com/JanetBarresiOK/videos/1089724958056950/?__xts__[0]=68.ARDi47F1zZWmbx4KwIti9k-ntuRCPymFJRWUrfL2JRJT8OQM40yy2gfmqne28KoEltMYEsYGsLyIx8Ih7X_Aj5kvyZWBFn7k1upK2K2YGesf4oSwc1XYGBNNxWdzVZYTRk1pnJbCjgpJr8gGM02QceSogs7-6PSxpCv_b81sXLy3r_Cb-LuqihTER9XVhRzlbonAT-b5gRMsQ8mr6NFhdWNlDs088rYIV-vqA6DyorQnOo2RpBm8hcvHOLuFQffsOJAEbnX2I-bU6tleXKJAFzvAW5WG0FWBFUB37Tg6BG1V3Sp_7KyXedIdVjcCQ_S-zNJ2KfTS_nHiiQ4iV61OzX-aRb2pIDHPXuw&__tn__=-R) on our campaign. As we all know, these are indeed unique and difficult times. The COVID-19 virus threatens us all -- with my decades of experience as a health care provider, I am keenly aware of the risk of transmission of the virus to each of us and our loved ones. I urge you to heed the recommendations of President Trump, his expert task force and the CDC.

We are all settling in to the new normal and each of us prays that this time of self-quarantine and social distancing will be very short. Your health, your family’s health and the health of all Oklahomans is paramount. That is why I wanted to inform you of some necessary changes we are instituting for our campaign. We have taken steps to suspend all in-person campaigning and fundraising appointments. In addition, all fundraising events are postponed until that time when we are advised that it is safe to resume normal activities. I will still be pursuing a vigorous schedule of outreach to Oklahomans in the 5th Congressional District but I will do it using electronic and virtual communication.

Those of you that know me understand that I am not a fan of "keeping my distance" from voters. I enjoy meeting you, shaking your hand and listening to your thoughts about the serious issues facing our country. However, we have a serious challenge facing all of us. From what I know of spending a lifetime in Oklahoma, raising a family and operating a successful small business, I am privileged to see the strength and resiliency of Oklahomans. I have no doubt we will make it through this together and come out on the other side better and stronger as a state and as individuals.

I want to encourage you to sign up for my emails on my website, Janet2020.com, and to follow and like us on Facebook to get updates. So, until I get to greet you in person again I will lift you and your family up in prayer for your safety health and well-being. Take care and talk to you soon.

Best,

body:
[1]Powered By People

We all want to do our best in the midst of this pandemic. We thought we’d
put together a few ways that you can help.

First, there are a lot of people who need help getting food for themselves
and their families:

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42405?t=1001&akid=82047%2E444211%2EjIczmH ]Can you donate to food banks that are responding to COVID-19 by
providing food to those in need? Support Feeding America here.

Donate

We are grateful to everyone doing their best, staying in and away from
public gatherings, keeping your distance from others, helping the older
and more vulnerable in your lives, and being kind and thoughtful.

We also want to share these two useful resources:

* [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42406?t=1002&akid=82047%2E444211%2EjIczmH ]Read this great advice from health care leaders. We can save lives
by staying home.

* [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42407?t=1003&akid=82047%2E444211%2EjIczmH ]Look at this clear explanation for why we’ve got to keep our
distance from each other right now.

Thank you so much for showing up for each other in this tough moment.

All of our best wishes for health and safety,

The Powered by People Team

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=82047%2E444211%2EjIczmH
